content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Administration 
322-5660 
Executive/Legal 
322-5901 
March 27, 1984 
Emerson Hall 
District Superintendent 
Keppel Union School District 
351lB North 82nd Street East 
Littlerock, CA 
93543 
Enforcement 
322-6.oUl 
Advice Letter No. A-84-064 
Dear Mr. Hall: 
Statements of Economic Int_st 
322-6444 
This letter is to confirm my oral advice to you. 
You stated 
that you are the District Superintendent for the Keppel Union 
School District. As your question concerns a third party's 
responsibilities under the Act, I can only provide you with 
general advice. 
FACTS 
Your District employs both a husband and wife as teachers. 
The wife is also the president of the teachers' union, a 
position for which she is not compensated. 
The husband is 
running for a position on the School Board. 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
Does the Political Reform Act prevent the husband from 
assuming a position on the School Board? 
GENERAL DISCUSSION 
The Political Reform Act does not prevent an individual from 
assuming a governmental position. 
However, you should check 
with your City Attorney or the District Counsel to see if any 


Emerson Hall 
March 27, 1984 
Page 2 
other law might restrict a teacher from holding a position 00 
the School Board. 
The Act may affect an official's 
decisionmaking. 
Government Code Section 8710011 prohibits a 
local public official from making, participating in the making, 
or in any attempting to use his official position to influence a 
decision~1 in which he knows or has reason to know that he has 
a financial interest. 
An official has a "financial interest" in 
a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision 
will have a material financial effectll on: 
* * * 
(c) 
Any source of income • • • aggregating two hundred 
fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to ••• the 
public official within 12 months prior to the time when the 
decision is made •••• 
(Section 87103(c) .) 
Under the Act, an official's private employer becomes a 
source of income. 
In addition, his spouse's private employer 
normally becomes a "source of income" because the official has a 
community property interest in his spouse's income. 
(Section 
82030.) 
However, if the official or his spouse is employed by a 
local government entity, such as a school district, the salary 
received is not considered to be "income" under the Act. 
(Section 82030 (b) (2) .) 
Therefore, the official's disquali-
fication is not required on decisions materially affecting 
either the governmental entity, or the salary of the employee or 
his or her spouse. 
11 Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. 
II For the definitions of these phrases, see the enclosed 
copy of 2 cal. Adm. Code Section 18700. 
31 
For an explanation of the phrase "material financial 
effect," see the enclosed copy of 2 cal. Adm. Code Section 
18702. 


Emerson Hall 
March 27, 1984 
Page 3 
If I can provide you with any additional information, please 
feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosures 
very truly yours, 
-11ffl·s~~~~ 
Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.facts:
Your District employs both a husband and wife as teachers. 
The wife is also the president of the teachers' union, a 
position for which she is not compensated. 
The husband is 
running for a position on the School Board.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a teacher whose spouse is employed as a teacher and union president at the same school district can assume a position on the School Board without violating the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prevent the husband from assuming the School Board position; however, disqualification rules under Government Code Section 87100 may apply to certain decisions. Specifically, an official must recuse themselves from decisions materially affecting their governmental employer or spouse's salary, but government employment income is exempt from the 'financial interest' definition under Section 82030(b)(2).
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: Document follows standard FPPC advice letter format with FACTS, QUESTION PRESENTED, and GENERAL DISCUSSION sections. No formal CONCLUSION section present, but conclusion drawn from discussion text. Statutory references and enclosures mentioned. One apparent typo in enforcement number (322-6.oUl).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Political Reform Act prevent the husband from 
assuming a position on the School Board? 
GENERAL DISCUSSION 
The Political Reform Act does not prevent an individual from 
assuming a governmental position. 
However, you should check 
with your City Attorney or the District Counsel to see if any 


Emerson Hall 
March 27, 1984  
other law might restrict a teacher from holding a position 00 
the School Board. 
The Act may affect an official's 
decisionmaking. 
Government Code Section 8710011 prohibits a 
local public official from making, participating in the making, 
or in any attempting to use his official position to influence a 
decision~1 in which he knows or has reason to know that he has 
a financial interest. 
An official has a "financial interest" in 
a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision 
will have a material financial effectll on:

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not prevent the husband from assuming the School Board position; however, disqualification rules under Government Code Section 87100 may apply to certain decisions. Specifically, an official must recuse themselves from decisions materially affecting their governmental employer or spouse's salary, but government employment income is exempt from the 'financial interest' definition under Section 82030(b)(2).

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter determining that the Political Reform Act does not inherently prevent a teacher whose spouse works at the same school district from serving on the School Board, but noting that disqualification rules under Section 87100 may restrict certain decisions where financial interests exist.
